Title: Is floral design easy? Design a flower arrangement using a flower frog a simple design tool #flowers
Description: Join me in this flower arrangement tutorial where I show you how to create a beautiful modern garden style arrangement using a flower frog. You might be wondering, is floral design easy? Well, with my step-by-step instructions, you'll be amazed at how simple it is to create a stunning floral arrangement. We start with bellflowers to create the structure, then use a garden rose as the focal point, add some spray roses to fill out the shape, and ranunculus to create movement. To fill in the holes, we add more roses with a beautiful scent and finish off with star of Bethlehem that will continue blooming day after day. Follow along and learn how to create your own gorgeous flower arrangement today! #flowerarrangement #flowertutorial #easycenterpiece #floralstyling #diyflower #floraldesign

Title: Flower Circus at Elmia Garden 🇸🇪 Floral Trends 2026 & Inspiration from Sweden

Explore the latest floral trends, stunning flowers like lilies, orchids, lisianthus, hydrangeas, and chrysanthemums, plus creative designs from the Floral Collective. Discover Swedish interior trends, eco pots, the Flower Grand Prix competition, and much more!

Title: Flower arrangement ideas & details for summer and fall 2024 | Bold Pink & Orange #flowerarrangement
Description:
Best current color inspiration? Flower arrangement ideas for summer and fall 2024 | Bold Pink & Orange Decorations.

wondering decorating your home office or for thanksgiving, birthday and any other ceremonies. How about implementing bold pink and orange colors? Here's why ⤵️⤵️⤵️

Energizing Celebrations: The Power of Bold Colors
Weddings and Beyond: Fresh flowers have graced weddings for centuries, adding a timeless touch to celebrations. As we emerge from the pandemic, couples are seeking ways to infuse energy into their special day.
Color Palettes: While neutrals remain chic, bold and thoughtful color palettes are stealing the spotlight. Pink and orange, whether in bouquets, arrangements, or installations, energize the atmosphere. Couples are daring to step beyond beige and embrace something brighter, more invigorating.
Unique Personality: These pops of color infuse weddings with personality. Each bloom tells a story a color story. Whether it’s a fiery sunset orange or a playful bubblegum pink, these hues create a memorable backdrop for love.
Texture and Statement Blooms
Monochromatic Sophistication: Neutral color schemes are timeless, but floral designers are playing with texture and scale. Monochromatic bouquets, where blooms share a color, still shine. However, it’s the variation in size and shape that keeps them fresh and unique.
Maximalist Aesthetics: From large-scale arrangements to bold color palettes, weddings are embracing maximalism. The more colors and patterns, the better! Trust your designer to create something interesting and beautiful.
Textural Blooms: Even within neutral palettes, texture matters. Think velvety petals, spiky thistles, and feathery foliage. These textural elements add depth and intrigue.
2024 Floral Design Trends: Predictions from Industry Pros
Celebrating Life: Floral arrangements in 2024 celebrate life itself. They burst forth with vibrancy and festivity.
Warm and Exuberant Palette: Pink, red, magenta, orange, and bright yellow dominate. These colors evoke joy, passion, and celebration.
Nature Connection: Whether it’s through wildflower-inspired designs or organic shapes, there’s a deeper connection to nature. Floral arrangements feel alive and untamed.
Color Theory in Floral Design
Bold Cornerstone Colors: Red, yellow, and blue form the foundation. These primary colors allow artists to mix any other shade. They’re the vibrant building blocks.
Pink and Orange: Pink exudes romance and softness, while orange brings warmth and zest. Together, they create a harmonious contrast a dance of passion and energy.
Timeless Appeal: These hues transcend trends. They evoke memories of sunsets, citrus groves, and childhood popsicles. That nostalgia keeps them perennially popular.

Let’s talk about color trends for 2024 🌼🌿

The team recently visited one of our favorite places in NYC, Dutch Flower Line. Every time we visit, we’re instantly immersed in a beautiful world of blooms and greenery, and we can feel our creativity blossoming! 

While there, we wanted to talk about Pantone’s Color of the Year. For 2023, it’s been Viva Magenta, which is described as powerful and empowering, and we saw this color incorporated into many weddings over the last couple of months. 

As 2023 comes to a close, we’re leaving Viva Magenta behind and looking ahead to 2024 and what we expect to be a year full of warm hues like browns and oranges, based on the color trends we saw during New York City Fashion Week 😍

Title: The main wedding trends of 2024-2025
Description:
💍💭The main wedding trends of 2024-2025:

 1 Minimalist Bridal Gowns – Simple yet elegant dresses with clean lines, perfect for the modern bride. 👰🏼‍♀️ 
 2 Floral Arches – Using dried flowers, pampas grass, and greenery to create stunning, eco-friendly floral arches. 🌾
 3 Eco-Friendly Decor – Sustainable choices such as reusable materials, earthy tones, and natural elements like wood and candles. 🌱
 4 Outdoor Venues – Intimate garden settings with string lights and a natural vibe.🌵
 5 Aerial Drone Footage - Couples are increasingly incorporating drone footage into their wedding videos. This provides stunning aerial views of the venue, creating cinematic and unforgettable wedding films. 🎥 
 6 Non-Traditional Wedding Cakes - Wedding cakes are getting creative with designs and flavors. From artistic, sculptural cakes to dessert tables with donuts, macarons, or pies, couples are reimagining the traditional cake with unique touches. 🎂 

These trends reflect a shift towards personalization, sustainability, and a balance between timeless elegance and modern flair. They will help create a romantic and modern atmosphere at any wedding! 🩷

Title: Decor Trends that are OUT for 2024 #modernfarmhouse #decor #decortrends #homedecor2024
Autumn Inspired Christmas Tree 🍁
Description:
So... 🍁

It's officially fall... and this tree is a physical representation of my mindset right now. I'm feeling the season - but through the lens of Christmas!

So many of you ask me about creating Thanksgiving trees - Christmas trees that are heavily influenced in design by your bountiful autumn decor.

Let me be the first to remind you that you can - and should! - do absolutely whatever brings you joy! 

But also - of course! Lots of you decorate your tree with fall elements, then transition it to more of a traditional Christmas palette after Thanksgiving.

What do you do in your home and spaces?

Simple Guide to Sustainable Flower Arrangements for Weddings for 2024 : Easy Tips to Reduce Costs and Environmental Impact.
You are considering flower arrangement ideas, flower business, form flower making,  outdoor wedding decor inspiration and indoor wedding decor inspiration.


✨How others are doing? 
Hire Eco-Friendly Vendors: Choose vendors who prioritize sustainability in their practices, such as using biodegradable products and reducing waste.
Select a Sustainable Venue: Optimize for venues that have eco-friendly policies, such as energy-efficient lighting and waste reduction programs.
Use Local and Seasonal Products: This reduces the carbon footprint associated with transportation and supports local businesses.
Rent or Buy Pre-Owned Attire: Renting wedding outfits or buying pre-owned dresses can significantly cut down on waste and costs.
Eco-Friendly Invitations: Consider digital invitations or use recycled paper for printed ones.

👑Boost  your creativity by: 
Sustainable Decorations: Use biodegradable or reusable decorations, and consider incorporating natural elements like plants and flowers.
Minimize Food Waste: Plan your menu carefully to avoid excess food, and consider donating leftovers to local shelters.
Green Transportation: Encourage guests to carpool or use public transportation to reduce emissions.
Give Back: Instead of traditional favors, make a donation to an environmental charity on behalf of your guests.

💡Tips: Choose Seasonal and Local Produce: Use fruits and flowers that are in season and locally sourced to reduce the carbon footprint.
Edible Flowers: Incorporate edible flowers like pansies, nasturtiums, and marigolds. They add vibrant colors and are safe to eat.
Seedable Wedding Invitations: These are made from biodegradable paper embedded with seeds. After the wedding, guests can plant the cards and grow flowers, herbs, or vegetables. This not only reduces waste but also gives guests a lasting memory of your special day.

2025 predictions for wedding flower trends #shorts
Description:
My prediction is we’ll see this years florals reflect a mix of creativity, sustainability, and bold individuality, making 2025 an exciting year for wedding florals. Here are my 5 top predictions for the year of wedding flowers:

###**1. Fruits & Veggies**
From citrus accents to lush greenery and even artichokes, fruits and veggies add texture, color, and a natural charm to your big day. Who says blooms should have all the fun!

### **2. Smaller, Minimal Bouquets**  
Posy-style bouquets featuring fewer blooms or even a single statement flower for an understated, elegant vibe.  Roses, calla lilies, peonies, and proteas are great choices for this style.

### **3. Monochromatic Themes**  
Bouquets and decor using one color in varying shades (e.g., a gradient of pinks or blues) for a sophisticated, cohesive look.  Another variation on this theme is having each bridesmaid have a different flower choice, and the bridal bouquet incorporating all the flowers in her bouquet. 

### **4. Modern and Sculptural Florals**  
Unique blooms like anthurium, protea, and calla lilies give contemporary appeal. Texture is reining supreme and unique flowers are stealing the show.

### **5. Cascade Bouquets**  
Lavish, flowing bouquets with trailing greenery, adding romance and grandeur.  These bouquets were out of vogue for a while, but they are back now with a modern twist!

Description:
Here’s how I wrapped this Valentine’s Day floral bouquet using flowers that I got from Walmart❤️🌹✨. This is a pretty easy bouquet wrapping tutorial, so if you want to see how to wrap a bouquet in paper this is for you 💐✨

I used:
💖 four packages of fake scented roses - Walmart
💖 5 Sheets of floral wrapping paper - Amazon

Many of you know Terry as a gardener, but did you know his day job is designing fabulous events and weddings?! Terry's business, The English Garden, is semi-famous (or maybe infamous - just kidding) here in Nashville for creating the most beautiful events! I guess we shouldn't limit it to Nashville, because in his 30+ year career as the owner of The English Garden, he has adorned events all over the United States... and the world! His elegant simplicity mixed with a little whimsy captivates the guests as soon as they walk in! In this video, Terry visits his flower wholesaler to check out their stock for an upcoming event. Title: From Pinterest to Petals: Turning 2026 Trends into Flowers with Ellie LaFever
Description:
Episode 314: Ellie’s back as the resident trend predictor, and this episode is all about what’s coming next for 2026, before your customers start asking for it. Lori, Vonda, and Ellie dig into Pinterest Predicts 2026 (yes, the data-driven one) and compare it to floral-industry trend insights, pulling out what actually matters for florists and flower shop owners. From the internet’s strong opinions on the “color of the year,” to cool blue taking over everything, to vintage details making a comeback, to scent-stacking, playful candy-colored ideas, and even the surprise “cabbage” moment - this one is packed with inspiration you can translate into real designs and real marketing. The big takeaway: you don’t have to chase every trend, just pick a few, use them strategically, and stay one step ahead.

'Trends through Community' - Trend Summit 2020 feat. Debra Prinzing of Slow Flowers
Description:
Debra Prinzing of Slow Flowers talks about community building within the flower industry and coming together to build trends.

We are thrilled to have Debra on board as a Featured Speaker and Contributor at Trend Summit 2020!  Debra Prinzing is the founder behind the 'Slow Flowers' Movement, she pens the monthly 'Slow Flowers Journal' in Florists' Review Magazine.  She hosts the annual Slow Flowers Summit, as well as the her Podcast, the 'Slow Flowers Podcast'.  Her farm to florists oeuvre provides such tremendous insight to our industry, and we look forward to her input to the trend discussion in Vancouver next March!

Debra will be a key presence at Trend Summit 2020, from being a contributor and speaker at our Trend Conference, to presenting her Slow Flowers Forecast at the Symposium Stage Show on Day 4!

______________________________________________________________

Trend Summit 2020 is a 6 Day Summit beginning with a 2 Day Trends Conference, followed by 4 Hands-On Workshops with the likes of Holly Chapple, Susan McLeary, Françoise Weeks, Gregor Lersch (not to mention 2 Spectacular Speaker Programs!).

Make sure to mark down March 12-17, 2020 on your calendars for this can’t miss event!!!

Elevate Your Wedding! Custom Floral Trends 2024
Description:
In this episode of Wedding Mayhem Podcast, hosts Sharon and Lauren chat with the incredibly talented florist Deborah, the creative mind behind Duchess & Ivy Floral Design. Known for her luxe, hand-crafted faux floral arrangements, Deborah shares how her brand was born from a passion for luxury and a keen eye for detail after spending 15 years in 5-star global hospitality.

Deborah discusses how she identified a gap in the wedding market for high-quality, affordable floral décor. With experience working with top-tier suppliers in the hotel industry, she sourced premium faux flowers to create Duchess & Ivy, a brand that combines the realism of fresh blooms with the elegance of luxury design. These stunning floral arrangements are offered on a rental basis, providing couples with a cost-effective, hassle-free solution for adding opulence to their big day.

Additionally, Duchess & Ivy has elevated its collections with the addition of a unique faux floral fragrance. Inspired by Moroccan Rose Petals, Oud, and Patchouli, this signature scent gives their faux flowers an aromatic richness, filling the room with the essence of fresh blooms.

Join us as Deborah takes us through her journey of building Duchess & Ivy, creating sustainable, luxury décor that redefines floral elegance in the wedding industry.

Title: Flowers Made Of Toilet Paper 👀 @diyhandmade77
Description: #shorts #viral #creative #diy #flowers #trending This girl went viral for creating a beautiful bouquet of flowers with only toilet paper, and it is way easier than you expect. First, she folds in the top corner two times, before starting to twist the paper along the top. This will give the flowers some very nice leafs, that you will be able to see in the end. Once the entire top is twisted, she moves onto rolling the paper into a rose. Then, she adds a rubber band and a stick for extra durability and detail. To finish off, she collects a bunch of flowers, and ties them together with a nice fabric and ribbon. Isnt this amazing?

Title: The easiest way to arrange tulips #tulip #florist #flowerarrangement  #flowerarranging #flowers
TULIP TIPS! 🌷#flowerhacks #tipsandtricks #easyhack
Description:
Tulips have to be one of my favorite flowers! I have found that they really don’t last very long though and it’s only a day or two before then begin to droop which sometimes is unavoidable if the flower is older when you get it but a trick that has worked for me is as soon as I get fresh tulips I poke a hole with a pin at the top of the stem. I did a ton of research on different methods and the pinhole allows the water from the vase to reach the top of the flower easier which helps keep your tulips standing upright and not drooping.

There are also some claims that adding a penny to the water of the vase helps with keeping your flowers fresh longer. It’s better to use an older penny (ones from before 1982) since after that they started just using a copper coating. Hope these tips help!

Title: DIY Tulip Bouquet made of fuzzy wire handmade #tulip #handmadebouquet #fuzzywire #bouquet
Tulip Trick #shorts #tulips
Description:
Here's how I get tulips to stand up straight.
 
Tulips tend to bend and droop but make them stand tall by doing these simple steps: 1. Cut an inch off the bottom of the stems at a diagonal 2. Fill a vase with only 2 inches of cold water, about the width of 3 fingers. 3. Drop a copper penny or two in the water (I only used one) then arrange the tulips in the vase and watch what happens…
 
After anywhere from a couple hours to overnight they go from curved and droopy to standing straight and tall. Mine greeted me in the morning with a salute.. Simply gorgeous!
 
Change the water every other day to keep your tulips standing tall.

Title: DIY pipe cleaner tulips flower #handmade #diy #gift #diyflower #diycrafts #flowercraft #fyp #foryou
Tips to help your tulips last longer #spring
Description:
Who else is thinking of SPRING 🌷
Tulips will bring spring into your home.
Here are some tips to help your them last longer.
•buy them closed to assure they’re fresh.
•Snip the bottoms diagonally at least 1/2 inch.
•remove the leaves that will rest below the waterline to avoid rot.
•make a pin hole just below each flower.. it allows air to escape and water to move up the stem.
•tulips love shallow cold water so add a few ice cubes.
•replace the water daily and add an old copper penny. (before 1982) It acts as a fungicide to naturally kill bacteria.
•also trim a bit off the bottom each day
Keep them in a cool place away from direct sun and your tulips will stay beautiful longer🌷💐 #tulips #care #spring

Title: FLOWER ARRANGEMENT IDEAS 477.  Red Tulips and Goldenrod.
Description:
In this design, we have red tulips and goldenrod for the fillings. 

A.  @Lotus Flower Arrangement, we do not use “floral foam”.   Instead, we use  natural wooden branches, bamboo branches, twigs, and leaves from the plants themselves as the bases for the flowers to stand and to support.  This is challenging.   However, it is the most important and interest part of the whole design.   Moving forward “kenzan”  is our next choice to use on  Ikebana flowers.  


B.  In terms of color matching and contrasting:  Example in good matching,  lighter purple are easily matched with darker purple.   In contrast, the most greenest plants and leaves are good contrast with flowers of all colors.

Amaryllises

Title: repotting dendrobium orchid plant with short tutorial method 🌱🌵 🌴🌵🌴🌻🌿🌿🌱🌲🌲#orchid #plants
What to do after your orchid is done blooming #orchid #phalaenopsis
How I take care of my orchids  #orchidcare #plantcaretips #orchidblooms #loveplants
Description:
And if lived in florida, I could grow these on trees and call it a day 😞

But since I’m in Indiana, we result to caring for orchid the basic way. The biggest tip is, if your orchid is healthy (no rott or mushy/dying of leaves, or severe plant issues) then there is no need to repot just yet. 

Also, a mistake I’ve seen is potting and orchid in soil. Orchids have those very thick roots and they will not survive in soil. My potting medium of choice is either orchid bark on its own or a combination of orchid bark and spaghnum moss. 

Stay tuned for an upcoming orchid video where I show you what to do once the blooms are gone.

Title: DIY Living Wall Frames using Air Plants and Orchids!
Description:
If you are looking for the preserved dyed moss we used in this video, check the link below (You may need more than one bag, depending on the size of frame you go with):
https://www.amazon.com/SuperMoss-25320-Forest-Moss-Preserved/dp/B00I6AJKI4/ref=sr_1_6?dchild=1&keywords=preserved+forest+moss&qid=1611955329&sr=8-6

Most of the other components and tools should be straightforward and can be found on Amazon or your local/craft stores. When choosing a wire mesh, we recommend that the holes of the grid are not too large. The one we used in the video has 1/2 inch holes.

Air plants (Tillandsia) are great for beginners and a bit easier to attach to the wire mesh. Below are the specific plants we used in the video, but there are an assortment of air plants or miniature orchids that can work!

The plants featured in this video:
Laelia dayana:
https://www.orchidweb.com/orchids/cattleya-alliance/species/laelia-dayana-aka-cattleya-bicalhoi-hartford-am-aos-x-var-coerulea
Tillandsia bergeri:
https://www.orchidweb.com/orchids/miscellaneous-non-orchids/tillandsia-bergeri
Tillandsia incarnata:
https://www.orchidweb.com/orchids/miscellaneous-non-orchids/tillandsia-incarnata
Search for all of our current available Tillandsia:
https://www.orchidweb.com/search.php?search=tillandsia&section=content

This video also accompanies a blog you can read here:
https://www.orchidweb.com/blog/dont-panic-something-is-growing-on-your-wall/

I recommend hand washing the crochet projects in cold water and a natural soap (Method Concentrated Laundry Liquid Detergent), rinse it out gently and lay flat on a towel to dry. The same for both cotton and acrylic yarns. I do not recommend warm water.

Title: 5 Simple Tips for Longer-Lasting Sunflower Bouquets #sunflowers  #BouquetTips #FlowerCare
Description:
🌻 5 Simple Tips for Longer-Lasting Sunflower Bouquets 🌻 Share your favorite sunflower tips below!
1. Keep stems long for flexibility, but trim them at a 45-degree angle to help them absorb water better. 
2. Remove any leaves that will be submerged in water, and tidy up by cutting off any damaged ones. 
3. Use room temperature water in your vase - just right, not too hot or cold. 
4. Place your sunflowers in a bright spot in your home, but avoid direct sunlight to keep them fresh. 
5. Change the water every other day and give the stems a quick trim to keep them looking their best...............

🌻 How to Preserve a real Sunflower | Hanging Upside Down Drying Method
Description:
Sunflowers are beautiful but also one of the trickiest flowers to preserve because of their thick stems and heavy center. In this video, I’m showing you how I preserved my sunflower by hanging it upside down.

Steps & Tips:
1️⃣ Trim off extra leaves.
2️⃣ Tie a string around the stem.
3️⃣ Hang it upside down in a dark, dry, ventilated place.
4️⃣ Wait 2–3 weeks until it feels papery.

💡 Tip: Sunflowers may bend or lose petals while drying so don’t worry if it’s not “perfect.” The beauty is in the natural dried look!

💡 Sunflowers don’t always dry perfectly, but that’s part of their rustic charm!

HOW TO: Preserve your peonies! #flower #tipsandtricks #summerflowers
Description:
The ones I wrapped are past their prime for this, but I’m trying anyway. However, next year I’ll do it right! Here is how to preserve your peonies so you can enjoy them for up to two months!  

1. You must cut them when the green outer layer has opened and the first couple of petals have just begun to unfurl. The bud should feel like a firm marshmallow when pressed from the top.  
2. Give a good amount of length and remove all leaves except the top one or two to preserve energy. 
3. Make sure that the peonies are COMPLETELY free from moisture and roll them carefully in a paper towel. Cover the buds with a plastic grocery bag and tie. Do the same with the stem side. 
4. Keep in refrigerator for up to two months. Pull some out when you want a fresh bouquet. Check on them weekly to make sure they are doing ok! 
5. Give the stems a fresh diagonal cut and arrange in a water filled container. 
Have you done this before? Did it work for you? Any other peony gardening tips you want to share? Leave them below!  
 
Also, did I say it right??? I didn’t say Pee-OH-nee! 😂

Description:
Beautiful Hydrangea in My Garden 🌸💗💚 #shorts #hydrangeas #trending

Hydrangea, (/haɪˈdreɪndʒiə/) commonly named the hortensia, is a genus of more than 70 species of flowering plants native to Asia and the Americas. By far the greatest species diversity is in eastern Asia, notably China, Korea, and Japan. Most are shrubs 1–3 m (3 ft 3 in – 9 ft 10 in) tall, but some are small trees, and others lianas reaching up to 30 m (100 ft) by climbing up trees. They can be either deciduous or evergreen, though the widely cultivated temperate species are all deciduous.

The flowers of many hydrangea act as natural pH indicators, sporting blue flowers when the soil is acidic and pink ones when the soil is alkaline

Hydrangea is derived from Greek and means ‘water vessel’ (from ὕδωρ húdōr "water" + ἄγγος ángos or αγγεῖον angeîon "vessel"), in reference to the shape of its seed capsules. The earlier name, Hortensia, is a Latinised version of the French given name Hortense, honoring French astronomer and mathematician Nicole-Reine Hortense Lepaute. Philibert Commerson attempted to name the flower Lepautia or Peautia after Lepaute. However, the flower's accepted name later became Hortensia. This led to people believing Lepaute's name was Hortense, but the Larousse remarks that this is erroneous, and that the name probably came from hortus, garden

Hydrangea flowers are produced from early spring to late autumn; they grow in flowerheads (corymbs or panicles) most often at the ends of the stems. Typically the flowerheads contain two types of flowers: small non-showy fertile flowers in the center or interior of the flowerhead, and large, sterile showy flowers with large colorful sepals (tepals). These showy flowers are often extended in a ring, or to the exterior of the small flowers. Plants in wild populations typically have few to none of the showy flowers, while cultivated hydrangeas have been bred and selected to have more of the larger type flowers.

There are two flower arrangements in hydrangeas with corymb style inflorescences, which includes the commonly grown "bigleaf hydrangea"—Hydrangea macrophylla. Mophead flowers are large round flowerheads resembling pom-poms or, as the name implies, the head of a mop. In contrast, lacecap flowers bear round, flat flowerheads with a center core of subdued, small flowers surrounded by outer rings of larger flowers having showy sepals or tepals. The flowers of some rhododendrons and viburnums can appear, at first glance, similar to those of some hydrangeas.

Hydrangea flowers, when cut, dehydrate easily and wilt very quickly due to the large surface area of the petals. A wilted hydrangea may have its hydration restored by first having its stem immersed in boiling water; as the petals of the hydrangea can also absorb water, the petals may then be immersed, in room-temperature water, to restore the flower's hydration

In this video, i shared a tutorial how to make lily flower from paper. You can find all supplies at home and make it easily. I used colored hvs paper to make it easier because you don’t have to coloured it anymore. But you can use plain hvs paper or even used paper like newspaper or old books and colored it with water based paint. Flower Bouquet | DIY Bell Flower Bunch Tutorial 🌸 | Creative Crochet Ideas
Description:
🌸 Welcome to my crochet corner! 🌸
In this step-by-step tutorial, I’ll guide you through making a beautiful Bell Flower Bunch — a charming crochet flower bouquet that’s perfect for home décor, gifts, or as a unique bag charm. This project is simple, fun, and full of creative crochet ideas you’ll love trying. 🧶💖

🔍 In this video, you’ll learn:
1️⃣ Introduction – What the Bell Flower Bunch is and how you can use it for decoration, accessories, or gifts.
2️⃣ Materials & Tools – 3mm yarn, 2.5mm crochet hook, scissors, yarn needle, and color combinations (green, white, pink).
3️⃣ Step-by-Step Instructions – Detailed stitches for the flower center, petals, stems, and mini bell flowers.
4️⃣ Assembly – How to attach the stems and flowers together for a neat, professional finish.
5️⃣ Finishing Touches – Adding loops for hanging or attaching to bags.

💡 Why you’ll love this project:
Easy enough for beginners, yet satisfying for advanced crocheters.
Customizable colors to match your style.
Perfect handmade gift idea. 🎁

📏 Crochet Pattern & Stitches Used:
Flower center: Magic Ring (MR), chains, double crochet (dc), half double crochet (hdc)
Petals: dc increases, slip stitch (slst)
Bell flowers: magic ring, dc, chain stitches
Stem: chain + slip stitch for firmness

🧵 Materials Needed:
Yarn: Cotton 3mm (Green, Pink, White)
Crochet hook: 2.5mm
Scissors
Yarn needle

Title: How to Make a Carnation Flower | DIY Tissue Paper Flower
Description:
For Flower Lovers: How to Make a Carnation Flower Using Paper Towels

Supplies used:
 1. Paper Towels
 2. Watercolor Paints:
 • Petals: Pink and red
 • Stem: Army green
 3. PVA Multi-Purpose Glue
 4. Glue Gun
 5. A4 Paper: Half sheet
 6. Brush
 7. Scissors
 8. Thread

Step-by-step instructions:

 1. Prepare the Paper Towels:
 • Take 4 sheets of paper towel and fold them in half into a square shape.
 • Cut the folded paper towel, dividing it into 8 smaller pieces.
 2. Paint the Napkins:
 • Using watercolor, paint the napkins with red, yellow and pink colors.
 • After painting, gently squeeze out any excess water and hang the napkins to dry.
 • If you paint in the evening, the napkins will dry by morning without requiring additional waiting time.
 3. Create the Flower Petals:
 • Once the painted napkins are dry, stack all 8 pieces together and fold them in a zigzag (accordion) pattern.
 • Tie the center of the folded napkin with a thread to secure it.
 • Gently separate the layers of the napkin, creating a petal-like appearance.
 4. Make the Flower Stem:
 • Take a regular sheet of paper and cut it in half.
 • Use one half to form the stem by rolling or shaping it to your desired thickness.
 Assemble the Flower:
 • Use a glue gun to attach the paper stem to the base of the flower.

Description:

Lewis combined locally-grown dahlias with materials foraged in Editor-in-Chief Margot Shaw's garden to make a vibrant fall flower arrangement. Lewis gathered early-blooming sasanqua camellias, Russian sage, abelia, beautyberry, and nandina berries. Description:
🪻 Beautiful Crochet Lavender Heart Bouquet ❤️ | Easy Handmade Crochet Gift Idea


Welcome to my Channel 💜
In this tutorial, I’ll show you how to make a Beautiful Crochet Lavender Heart Bouquet, perfect for decoration, gifting, or DIY crafts.
It’s easy, elegant, and adds a lovely handmade touch to any space.
✨ Don’t forget to Like, Share & Subscribe for more crochet creations!

🧶 Materials Used:
Soft Cotton Yarn (Lavender, White, Green), 3.0 mm Hook, Yarn Needle, Satin Ribbon

❤️
Each heart is crocheted separately, then combined into a bouquet with green stems and a soft ribbon bow.
It gives a gentle lavender theme — perfect for room decor, gifting, or photo props.

🌸 Where You Can Use This Crochet Heart Bouquet:

As a table or shelf decoration

For home or room décor

In gift hampers or handmade gifts

As a photo prop for craft or wedding setups

For Valentine, Friendship, or any special occasion

As a cute bouquet for display or keychain idea

Title: 4 Tips to Get Marvelous Garden Mums (Chrysanthemums)
Description:
To get the most marvelous garden mums (chrysanthemums), here are 4 tips to consider:

1. Purchase garden mums that are in tight bud form. This way you can enjoy the show as they bloom and enjoy the flowers for longer. 
2. Place your garden mums in a sunny location out of the rain.
3. Water your garden mums every other day. They like to be moist but not waterlogged. Water around the base of the plant, avoiding overhead watering, as wet foliage can lead to fungal diseases.
4. Deadhead the flowers regularly for repeat blooms.

Follow these tips and be rewarded with magnificent mums for months on end!

Flower Arrangement Tutorial. 

B.  @LOTUS FLOWER ARRANGEMENT:  
We use  natural wooden branches, bamboo branches, twigs, and leaves from the plants themselves as the bases for the flowers to stand and to support.  This is challenging.   However, it is the most interest part of the whole design.   Moving forward “kenzan”  is our next choice to use on  Ikebana flowers.  

C.  COLOR MATHCHING & CONTRASTING:  
Example in good matching,  lighter purple are easily matched with darker purple.   In contrast, the most greenest plants and leaves are good contrast with flowers of all colors. 

D.  NAMES OF FLOWERS:

The Chrysanthemum is the flower of the month for November, and this beautiful flower comes with a wide variety of meanings all across the globe. In Asia the chrysanthemum signifies life and rebirth, making them great gifts for birthdays and baby showers. 



   A symbol of the sun, the Japanese consider the orderly unfolding of the chrysanthemum's petals to represent perfection, and Confucius once suggested they be used as an object of meditation. It's said that a single petal of this celebrated flower placed at the bottom of a wine glass will encourage a long and healthy life.

Inspiration behind The Chrysanthemum Interior Design - Spring Home Ideas
Description:
#interiordesign #spring #homedecor 

My love of British gardens has led me to explore a whole range of outdoor spaces, from the formal gardens of stately homes to my own Oxfordshire countryside garden and I've incorporated the shapes and shades of many well-loved favourites.

The chrysanthemum design features on many products, cushions, lampshades, fabrics, wallpapers, roller blinds and is even featured in our stationery collection. Chrysanthemums are a lovely hardy perennial plant that will continue to flower through the late summer months. My favourites are the Bright Yellow flowers produced above the dark green foliage.

I love putting patterns together so I would recommend pairing the chrysanthemum with Juneberry and bird, it's one of my favourite combinations, the yellows work so well together, alternatively, the yellows also look fabulous with the yellow colour in the Broom and bee sky fabrics.

If you want to make chrysanthemum the star of our room there are so many fabulous paints and fabrics which work really well with cotton linen fabric. I recommend looking at texture, velvets as they look so lovely next to the wide weave of the linen, and give a really rich colour. If you want to be brave, a bright yellow velvet chair would look beautiful but also deep greys would look luxurious alongside the floral print.

Check out my blog post to see more on how to style Chrysanthemum and other spring interiors.

Title: How to design s large bridal Bouquet w/ $Peony, Ranunculus & Sweet Pea #weddingflowers #iloveflowers
A Bridal Bouquet: Pink mink protea, white peonies, roses, ranunculus & magnolia | Wedding Flowers
Description:
I have been longing to get into this series for some time and apologize for the timely delay. There have been so many requests for bouquets, men’s button hole flowers, corsages, head garlands or floral crowns, church and table flowers and now is the time – I’m here to help.

Most of you asked for handheld bouquets, which are currently the preferred style and I must say the easiest to make. Personally, I still love a good wired bouquet which take hours of wiring, but gives a smaller handle and a lighter to carry completed bouquet.

Loose round and informal is the best way to describe this style of handheld bouquet. You could use fresh but as I’ve discovered from zooming into a recent International Floral Association & Flower
Growers conference, Covid has caused a worldwide storage and supply of many wedding blooms. All is not lost and I am so pleased with what I’ve created for http://www.prestigebotanicals.com. My intension was to create blooms that were as near to nature as possible and when nature couldn’t provide, then there would be an excellent alternative.

You will need:
- 2 pink mink proteas
- 3 white peonies
- 2 or 3 stems blush apricot pink ranunculus, 2 stems short white magnolia, 2 blush pink roses, 2 stems dusty miller & seed eucalyptus
- 3 medium sized arelia leaves. These are used for base support and framing of your finished bouquet.
- Cable ties to bind and secure stems.

Title: How to Care for Ranunculus Flowers  -Flower Moxie Product Video
Description:
Shop wholesale wedding flowers at https://flowermoxie.com/

Oh Ranunculus!  Me picking my favorite flower is like Sophie's Choice, but for me, they are my absolute favorite bloom!

Ranunculus Recap:
➡️  They typically come tightly closed so to expedite the opening process set them in a sunny spot!  ➡️ Warm water never hurts, too. 
 ➡️  Curves are normal!  Sometimes the stems are straight like a rose, but more often they are curved because the bud is heavier than what the stem can support.  
➡️  For REALLY CURVY or broken stems, you can always run thin floral wire up the center (it's hollow af) OR you can just wrap the wire around the stem if the stem is going to be hidden anyway.

How To Propagate Carnations
Description:
Today we have Karon teaching us how to propagate Carnations.
She begins by filling a plastic bottle (which has been cut in half) with some good-quality soil. Karon continues to snip the stem below one node and above the next along the stem. Now to do the next step you can either use honey or root compound. If you're using a rooting compound you must first dip the stem in water in order to pick up the powder (with honey there's no need to do so). Then make a hole in your soil for the stem to sit in. Spray the contents of the hothouse with water to allow condensation when the lid is on.
The next step is to wait for roots to grow and replant in your garden.
We would love to see how your Carnations turn out so make sure to post on Facebook and Instagram and tag us @westridgeflorist  :)

Title: Carnation Hack For Floral Arrangements
Description:
🌸 Love them or hate them, it’s time to embrace the carnation - and here’s my hot take! 🔥💐

While some may dismiss carnations as “ordinary” or “overused,” I firmly believe it’s time for a fresh perspective. Let me tell you why:

1️⃣ Unleash your creativity! 🎨 Think beyond the traditional bouquet and explore the untapped potential of carnations. With their diverse colors and sturdy petals, you can create mesmerizing floral arrangements that push the boundaries of floral artistry. 🌼

2️⃣ Defying the trends. 💁‍♀️ Embracing the carnation means standing out from the crowd. In a world filled with roses and tulips, choosing these underappreciated beauties demonstrates your unique sense of style and willingness to challenge the status quo. 💪🌺

3️⃣ A symbol of resilience. 💥 Carnations are survivors. They endure harsh conditions, blooming brightly even in the most challenging environments. By incorporating carnations into your life, you’re reminded of the power of resilience and the ability to flourish against all odds. 🌷

4️⃣ Nostalgia with a modern twist. 🕰️ Remember those nostalgic moments when you received your first carnation? By embracing these timeless flowers, you can infuse a touch of nostalgia into your present, connecting the past with the present in a uniquely beautiful way. 💫

So let’s break free from the stereotypes and give carnations the love and admiration they deserve! 💐 Share your thoughts below: are you ready to join me in the carnation revolution, or do you still need some convincing? Let’s discuss! 👇🌸

Title: Tips for a healthy and homemade 🌸🌼 FLOWER ARRANGEMENT
Description:
1- For the flowers: Choose the freshest flowers, trim dying leaves and petals, trim flowers at an angle, and cut the flowers in three lengths for the best arrangement (measure the shortest to fit the length of the vase).
2- For the vase:
- Start with a clean vase.
- Use the flower food that comes with your flowers or mix 1 tbsp white vinegar (inhibits bacterial growth) and 1 tbsp sugar (food for flowers) for every 2 cups of lukewarm water.
- Fill the vase about ⅓ full (too much water accelerates your flower’s life span).
- Refresh water daily.
- Keep out of direct sunlight.
3- Use floral or scotch tape strips to make a grid across the vase to help your arrangement stay in place.

Title: Lily and Carnation Flower Arrangement
Description: A lily and carnation flower arrangement combines the elegance of lilies with the fullness of carnations to create a stunning display. The large, graceful lilies serve as the focal point, adding height and sophistication, while the ruffled carnations provide texture and volume. To create balance, lilies can be arranged in the center, with carnations surrounding them in a circular pattern. Complement the flowers with greenery like eucalyptus or ferns to add depth and contrast. This versatile arrangement works beautifully as a centerpiece for weddings, special events, or to bring a touch of elegance to any room.

Artificial flowers, also known as faux flowers or silk flowers, are artificial replicas of real flowers made from various materials like silk, polyester, plastic, or other synthetic fabrics. These artificial blooms are designed to mimic the appearance of natural flowers, often with remarkable detail and craftsmanship.

Advancements in manufacturing techniques have made artificial flowers look more lifelike than ever before. They come in a wide range of colors, shapes, and sizes, imitating everything from common flowers like roses, lilies, and sunflowers to exotic blossoms like orchids and tropical blooms. Some artificial flowers are even scented to replicate the natural fragrance of real flowers.

The use of artificial flowers offers several benefits and advantages:

Long-lasting Beauty: Unlike real flowers that wither and die, artificial flowers retain their beauty indefinitely with minimal care. They are an excellent option for individuals who want to enjoy floral arrangements without worrying about their short lifespan.

Allergen-free: For those with allergies to pollen or certain flowers, artificial flowers provide an allergen-free alternative to enjoy the beauty of blooms without any adverse reactions.

Versatility: Artificial flowers can be used in various settings, from home decor and weddings to commercial displays and events. They are also popular for creating lasting memorials and graveside arrangements.

Low Maintenance: Artificial flowers require little to no maintenance. They don't need water, sunlight, or pruning, making them perfect for busy individuals or those lacking a green thumb.

Year-round Availability: Certain flowers are seasonal and may not be available year-round, but artificial flowers can be found in any season, allowing you to create your favorite arrangements anytime.

Cost-effective: While some high-quality artificial flowers can be more expensive upfront, they prove to be cost-effective in the long run since they do not need regular replacements like real flowers.

Artificial flowers have come a long way from their early days, and many people now appreciate the beauty and convenience they offer. Whether used in floral arrangements, home decorations, or special occasions, artificial flowers can add a touch of everlasting elegance and charm to any setting.

Title: Hydrangea Wedding Bouquet Ideas
Description:
Stunning Hydrangea Wedding Bouquet Ideas for Your Big Day
Your wedding bouquet is one of the most important floral elements of your celebration, and hydrangeas make a gorgeous choice! Known for their lush, full blooms and romantic appeal, hydrangeas add volume, texture, and elegance to any bridal bouquet. Whether you're dreaming of a classic, rustic, or modern wedding, hydrangeas can beautifully complement your theme.

Here are some hydrangea wedding bouquet ideas to inspire you:

1. Classic All-White Hydrangea Bouquet
For timeless elegance, opt for an all-white hydrangea bouquet. The large blooms create a soft, cloud-like effect, perfect for traditional, church, or ballroom weddings. Pair with pearl accents or a satin ribbon for extra sophistication.

2. Romantic Pink & Blush Hydrangeas
Soft pink and blush hydrangeas bring a dreamy, romantic touch. Mix them with roses, peonies, or ranunculus for a delicate, feminine bouquet. Ideal for garden and fairytale weddings!

3. Rustic Blue Hydrangea & Eucalyptus Bouquet
Blue hydrangeas symbolize gratitude and harmony, making them a meaningful choice. Combine them with eucalyptus, baby’s breath, or lavender for a rustic, boho-chic vibe—perfect for barn or outdoor weddings.

4. Moody Deep Purple & Burgundy Bouquet
For a dramatic and luxurious look, deep purple or burgundy hydrangeas add rich color. Pair them with dahlias, dark roses, or dried foliage for a bold, autumnal bouquet.

5. Mixed Hydrangea & Greenery Cascade
A cascading bouquet with hydrangeas, ivy, ferns, and trailing vines creates a breathtaking, fairy-tale effect. This style works beautifully for vintage or woodland-themed weddings.

6. Pastel Hydrangea & Wildflower Bouquet
For a soft, whimsical feel, mix hydrangeas with wildflowers like daisies, astilbe, and Queen Anne’s lace. This effortless style is perfect for spring and summer weddings.

7. Monochromatic Hydrangea Bouquet
Choose a single shade—like all-blue or all-lilac—for a sleek, modern look. The uniformity of hydrangeas makes a striking statement.

8. Dried Hydrangea Bouquet for Boho Weddings
Dried hydrangeas offer a vintage, earthy aesthetic. Their muted tones (dusty pink, mauve, or tan) work beautifully for boho, rustic, or minimalist weddings.

Why Choose Hydrangeas for Your Wedding Bouquet?
✅ Full & Luxurious – Their large blooms create a voluminous look.
✅ Versatile Colors – Available in white, pink, blue, purple, and green.
✅ Symbolic Meaning – Represent gratitude, heartfelt emotion, and unity.
✅ Budget-Friendly – Fewer stems are needed due to their size.

Tips for Using Hydrangeas in Your Bouquet
🔹 Keep Them Hydrated – Hydrangeas drink a lot of water, so mist them before the ceremony.
🔹 Pair with Complementary Flowers – Roses, peonies, and ranunculus enhance their beauty.
🔹 Consider Seasonality – Hydrangeas are most abundant in spring and summer.

Would you choose hydrangeas for your wedding bouquet? Let us know your favorite style in the comments! 💐✨

Are you thinking about ordering your wedding flowers from Costco? 

I was thinking about doing our centrepieces this way for the longest time but ultimately backed out. 

PROS
✅ save money

CONS
❎ more stress (what if they don’t arrive or arrive damaged and your wedding is in two days???)
❎ time consuming
❎ might not turn out well
❎ generally annoying (need temperature control, need space to put them all in buckets, dealing with transporting water and fragile flowers) 
❎ who will set them up while you’re getting ready for the best day of your life??

This is a sweet and simple Mother’s Day craft for kids. Make a beautiful paper flower bouquet. A fun, easy, and low-cost art project perfect for classrooms or at home! This adorable DIY Mother’s Day gift idea uses basic supplies you probably already have: construction paper, scissors, glue, and markers.

Title: Creating a vibrant yellow funeral wreath #floraltribute #funeralflowers #flowers #florist
Description:
Bright, gentle, and full of warmth 💛

This vibrant yellow wreath was created as a heartfelt tribute to a life filled with light and kindness. Each flower — from the golden roses and soft cream carnations to the cheerful chrysanthemums and delicate alstroemeria — was chosen to reflect joy, warmth and everlasting love.

Set on a lush bed of ruscus and fern, the circular shape symbolises eternity — a reminder that love never ends, even when someone we care for is no longer with us.

Watch as this wreath comes to life in our timelapse reel, flower by flower, placed with care and meaning.

This tribute was crafted on a 30cm wreath ring from @oasisfloralproductsuk the NAYLORBASE® Floral Base is 100% recycled and recyclable and the OASIS® Bio Floral Foam degrades 91% in 3 years in an anaerobic environment.

#FuneralWreath #YellowTribute #CelebrationOfLife #SimplyFlowersUK #TimelapseFloristry #FloristAtWork #MadeWithLove

Title: Creating a coffin spray from 4 individual posy arrangements #funeralflowers #funeralflorist
Description:
A tribute to share, a memory to keep 🤍

This beautiful funeral tribute is made up of four individual posy arrangements — each created with soft white blooms including roses, peonies, lilies and chrysanthemums, interwoven with eucalyptus and lush seasonal foliage.

Designed to sit seamlessly together on top of the coffin during the service, each posy can then be taken home by a family member as a lasting keepsake of love and remembrance.

Watch our timelapse reel to see one of these posies being made — a peaceful process filled with care, intention and quiet reflection.

This heartfelt tribute is crafted with love and care on a sustainable foundation using degradable foam in a compostable tray from @oasisfloralproductsuk

How to make open heart sympathy arrangement
Description:
This free DIY video will demonstrate how to make an open heart standing fresh flowers funeral arrangement. The instructions are easy to follow with tips, tricks, and trade secrets provided by a professional florist. I need to prepare a standing open heart with fresh flowers today. In particular, I will be using white roses, white chrysanthemum and pink roses. This type of flower arrangement is quite easy to make yourself, even without a years of floral design experience. This type of flower is commonly used for many purposes such as weddings, memorial  ceremonies and even for Valentine's Day. The main thing is to have a basic shape, for example, I have a wire frame in the shape of a heart about 24-25” in diameter. I will need to soak two blocks of the floral sponge oasis . When soaking a sponge, you just need to put it on the surface of warm water without pressing on it and allow it to absorb water itself, freely submerging. Otherwise, air pockets may remain in it and the flowers, once in them, will quickly wither. Then you need to cut the blocks so that they fit to fill the hollow space in the wire frame. Fill the space and wrap with plastic tape specially designed for this purpose. If there is none, you can use it for wrapping around  regular  cut garbage bags as we show in our other videos. It is enough to wrap it once, but if you plan to make a bulky, heavy and more voluminous wreath, then it is advisable to wrap it twice. Only then, may it be necessary to make cuts with a knife to be able to insert  flowers in the sponge, especially for flowers with soft stems. Watch this video tutorial to learn how to create a beautiful open heart flower arrangement.#funeralflowers #standingflower #sympathyflowers #flowersdiy #flowermaking #s#floraldesign 
Materials used:
Ivory or white roses 20-24stems 
Pink roses 20-24stems 
10-12 white chrysanthemums
Wire open heart frame 24” in diameter 
Baby’s breath, salal greens
Plastic wrapping tape
Poly satin ribbon 
Floral wire 
Floral tools

Title: How to make a Simple Round Flower Arrangement for Christmas
Description:
Lets learn to make a simple traditional round floral design using a red Poinsettia house plant with this step by step tutorial aimed at beginners. 🌿

In today's video, I'll demonstrate all the basic steps needed to make a very traditional, round arrangement using a beautiful Poinsettia plant thats available from any supermarket or florist shops.  You just need a basic selection of garden or shop bought foliage to make this delightful table arrangement for  this very popular look. 💐

Ideal for both beginners and seasoned florists, this tutorial covers essential techniques for achieving a balanced and eye-catching design. 🌿🌸

      -How to create an arrangement with a house plant.

Title: Mayesh Design Star: Unexpected Holiday Floral Arrangement
Description:
#MayeshDesignStar #holidayflowers #wholesaleflowers #floraldesign

In the last episode of her Mayesh Design Star series, Christy shows us two different holiday arrangements that focus on unexpected flowers for this special time of year. We all love seasonal flowers but here are some different and interesting alternatives this season. 

We would like to thank Christy and her team at Colonial House of Flowers for an amazing year as the Mayesh Design Star. We would also like to thank Justin Peay of Justin Peay Productions for making a beautiful video series for 2017. You are all incredible! 

More info & pictures: https://www.mayesh.com/unexpected-holiday-floral-arrangement/ 

Featured product 
Red arrangement: Freedom rose, Matador rose, burgundy dahlia, celosia, fiddlehead (aka monkey tail aka fern shoot), China fir, cedar, magnolia leaves,  jasmine vine, mini cymbidium, and ranunculus.

White arrangement: cedar, huckleberry, silver dollar eucalyptus, jasmine vine, white anemones, white ranunculus, bunny tail, Wendy spray rose, viburnum berries and astilbe. 


Materials used: Lida vase by Accent Decor (red arrangement), Chelsea vase by Accent Decor (white arrangement) and Oasis wire netting.

Videographer: Justin Peay Productions​
Photographer: Kelli Boyd Photography​
Host: Christy Hulsey, Colonial House of Flowers​

Title: 🌸 “My Mom Is…” Flower Craft for Mother’s Day | Easy DIY for Kids
Description:
Celebrate everything that makes Mom amazing! 💐 In this heartfelt Mother’s Day craft, Miss Daisy helps kids create a flower filled with kind words describing their mom — like “kind,” “strong,” “beautiful,” and “smart.” Each petal lifts to reveal a loving word underneath, making this a fun, interactive way for little ones to show their appreciation. A perfect handmade card or keepsake for Mother’s Day!

✨ Materials Needed:

Construction paper (various colors)

Scissors

Glue stick

Marker or pen

Smiling circle center for the flower

Positive adjectives to describe mom!

Make Mother’s Day extra special with this easy DIY kids craft!

Title: Mother's Day Flower Delivery: Coupons & Gift Ideas
Description:
FromYouFlowers.com is featured on Daytime with the best Mother's Day Flowers to send & a Mother's Day flower coupon. Visits http://www.fromyouflowers.com/Daytime for a flower coupon.

Cyndi: Mother's Day is the most popular holiday to send flowers--and it's only nine days away. Check out these beautiful arrangements that came from a company called From You Flowers that lifestyle expert Michelle Yarn has brought with her today. From You Flowers also has great gifts for every occasion you celebrate throughout the year. Michelle, good to have you here.
M: Hi.
C: Thanks for making our studio smell so darn good.
M: No problem. It smells fabulous, right?
C: Oh, beautiful.
M: I mean, it's a double whammy. Not only are they gorgeous, but it really does make your room smell amazing.
C: And it makes everyone just feel happy.
M: You feel special.
C: You do.
M: And you do call it--Mother's Day (coming up)--I would have never guessed that it's the most popular holiday to send flowers. But, if you are sending them throughout the year, FromYouFlowers.com is a great website--they have varieties for every occasion that you celebrate. So, I brought a few of their more popular arrangements for some of the occasions that we celebrate. Starting with the birthday.
C: The birthday!
M: Yeah!
C: Very good.
M: This one down here, you want something bright and cheery and fun. This is their Orange and Purple Explosion.
C: It's exploding too! With color.
M: It's beautiful. So, you've got some purple roses, some orange roses. You've got that alstroemeria in there--great mix, It starts at $34.99.
C: That's really good.
Michelle: Yeah, we're not breaking the bank here.
C: No, we're not.
Michelle: Very easy to do. Now, a really popular time to send flowers is your anniversary.
C: Yeah, don't forget it, first of all.
Michelle: Yeah, guys, we're going to help you with this one. You can never go wrong with roses. One dozen red roses--they're beautiful, they're classic. These start at $24.99
C: That's really affordable, for roses.
Michelle: I know. And if you really really want to wow her, you can send a dozen and a half. You can send two dozens. FromYouFlowers.com has lots of options for you there to do.
C: Very nice. And if you're in trouble, send flowers. It will also help you get out of trouble a little faster.
Michelle: Makes everything better, doesn't it?
C: It does, it does.
M: Another time you might send flowers--if someone is feeling under the weather--From You Flowers can send it to a home or a hospital. They will take care of that for you.
C: That's nice.
M: They have an arrangement. It's called One Thousand Blooms of Spring Lilies--it's gorgeous, gorgeous. And it's Peruvian lilies. You've got lots of variety of color in there. So, that's just another example. But you can see--so many options to choose from.
C: Spring flowers. May is tulip month.
Michelle: Tulips! Absolutely. That's what's in season, so if you want to go with that, it's a nice spring palette.
C: It just screams spring. I just hear birds singing, and it smells so good. And we mentioned Mother's Day, and we think of roses, but the purple rose--this is actually one of the most popular colors for roses right now.
M: Yeah, that's a great way to go. Purple is the Mother's Day color, as you can see what we're wearing here.
C: Yes, we're coordinated with the flowers!
M: And another fun thing--all their arrangements from FromYouFlowers.com come with a free personalized card. So, you can send a nice message, really make Mom or whoever you're sending it to smile. You can upgrade--if you want to add gifts like some chocolates, or a teddy bear or a balloon, it's all there for you. It's so easy.
C: Now, From You Flowers has a special on right now, for all our daytime viewers.
M: Yeah, it wouldn't be fun shopping if we didn't get a discount. So, on your next flower delivery, you can save 20%.
C: That's good.
M: Very good. All you have to do is go to FromYouFlowers.com/Daytime, and you can claim your 20% off coupon for your next flower delivery.
C: And we already know the prices are great, and they throw in the free card as well, so you really get to save money. And don't just wait for a special occasion to send flowers. I'll tell you--fellas--little tip: we women enjoy getting flowers when we least expect it.
M: We do. Send them to work too, because then, everyone else gets to see them (I'm just saying).
C: This just smells exquisite, and I know they last a long time as well.
M; They really do. It's beautiful. You can't go wrong.
C: Thank you, Michelle.
M: You're welcome.
C: And don't forget to visit FromYouFlowers.com/Daytime for a 20% off coupon, or go to DaytimeOnline.tv for more information. I think this goes nicely with your dress.
M: Well, thank you.
C: Thank you. It's a pleasure working with you.

Title: How To Make An Abstract Flower Arrangement With Minimal Flowers
Description:
How to make an abstract flower arrangement with minimal flowers 

Ingredients - flat bowl, old tubing ( we used the ribbed tubing from the vacuum cleaner ), kenzan or pinholder, calla lilies, anthuriums


I just can’t throw anything away - and that includes the vacuum cleaner tube that broke at the neck. Well it got fixed once so now replaced - but the tubing is interesting and goes well with this container. 
Visually it makes a strong statement, so bold flowers are the perfect accompaniment. We used these beautiful anthurium as they also have a sculptural feel, and the calla fit in perfectly as well. Yes, they are fresh, but this arrangement will make people look twice! 
You might love it or hate it - but flower designs should be able to make people question if they like it or not - to be seen and not overlooked. This design does just that.

Title: How To Make A Minimal Modern Floral Design
Description:
How To Make A Minimal Modern Floral Design Using Pinholder

Ingredients – allium stems x 5. Dracaena leaves x 2

These were grown in patch and I just saw them and realised they would make a great arrangement as they are already architectural and interesting. To show them off to the best, we have used a simple plastic dish set in a curved ‘s’ shape and to hold our flowers we have used two kenzans or pinholders. This arrangement is all about space and line and contrast, and just shows that you don’t need lots of materials to create something interesting.

Title: Chinese Flower Arrangement: Elegant & Minimalist #flowers
Description:
This is a floral arrangement.

• Main Flowers: It includes dahlias, clematis, chamomile, blue thistles, Marguerite daisies, thalictrum, green bellflowers, and fountain grass.

• Style and Uses: It presents a natural floral style as a whole, with colors mainly in purple and lavender, matched with white and green, looking fresh and elegant. It can be used for home decoration, placed in spaces such as the living room and study to add natural beauty and artistic atmosphere; it can also be used as a gift to convey romantic and elegant emotions.

• Cultural Background: Dahlias symbolize generosity and richness; clematis is known as the "queen of climbing flowers", implying nobility and beauty; chamomile represents gentleness and tranquility. The combination of these flowers is not only visually harmonious but also contains beautiful meanings, reflecting the florist's ingenious use of colors and the characteristics of the flowers.

Title: Modern Minimalist Flower Arrangement with Natural Vibes
Description:
🌸 
Simple flower arranging is the art of turning everyday blooms into personal, expressive creations. You don’t need advanced techniques — just a few basic principles of color harmony, balance, and composition can help you craft a beautiful and meaningful floral display.

Flower arranging is more than just decoration; it’s a calming, creative practice that allows you to slow down, connect with nature, and express yourself through petals and stems. Whether you're a total beginner or someone who enjoys working with flowers, this gentle art invites you to find beauty in simplicity and inspiration in every arrangement.

Clivia Miniata Flower Arrangement Ideas #flowerarrangementideas #flowers
Description:
Clivia miniata flower arrangements in a low bowl vase offer a striking and sophisticated display, perfect for adding a touch of elegance to any space. The bold, trumpet-shaped orange or red blooms of Clivia, paired with its dark green, strap-like leaves, create a vibrant contrast in a low, shallow vase. Arranging Clivia in a low bowl highlights the flower's lush, sculptural form, emphasizing a minimalist yet eye-catching style. Ideal for coffee tables, sideboards, or as a dining table centerpiece, these arrangements add warmth and a pop of color to modern and traditional interiors alike.

Ikebana Flower Arrangement #flowers #flowerarrangementideas
Description:
Ikebana Flower Arrangement #flowers #flowerarrangementideas


Ikebana Flower Arrangement Ideas:Minimalist Elegance: Focus on simplicity and balance. Use a few flowers, such as a single large bloom or a pair of delicate stems, to create a clean and sophisticated arrangement. Ensure the placement highlights the natural form of the flowers.Asymmetrical Balance: Embrace asymmetry by arranging flowers in a way that creates visual interest. Use varying heights and angles to achieve balance, making sure the arrangement feels dynamic yet harmonious.Seasonal Themes: Incorporate seasonal flowers and foliage to reflect the time of year. For example, use autumn leaves and chrysanthemums for fall, or cherry blossoms and greenery for spring. This approach connects the arrangement to the changing seasons.Natural Lines: Highlight the natural curves and lines of the stems and branches. Arrange them to follow their inherent shapes, creating a sense of flow and movement in the design.Color Harmony: Choose a color palette that complements the flowers and the space. Use analogous colors for a harmonious look or contrasting colors for a more striking effect. The key is to maintain balance and cohesion.Container Focus: Select a container that enhances the overall design. The container’s shape and material should complement the flowers and arrangement style, whether it’s a traditional vase or a modern, minimalist container.Zen Influence: Create a serene and meditative arrangement by using simple lines and a limited number of elements. Focus on the negative space and the interplay between the flowers and their surroundings to evoke tranquility.

How To Make A Simple Dried Flower Arrangement

Floral trends, chic new ways to bring the outside into your home

the first thing that you need to do is to get your Favorite Vessel and find different
earthy colors of dried flowers. Put the floral foam inside the vessel so that you can

Dried flowers were a big trend back in the 1980s, 
and although they fell out of favor in the early 2000s,
they are back in 2021 - PRESENT – and this time they are super stylish and beautiful.

Beautiful blooms are an exquisite and easy way to add life and color to your interiors. 
The top floral trends of 2021 are an exciting blend of juxtapositions – 
bold and bright versus neutrals with minimal styling.

‘The first trend we can expect to see in 2022 is a shift toward neutral shades, 
with soft colour palettes comprised of subtle tonal differences,’ says Lucia Polla, 
marketing manager at Serenata Flowers. 
‘As people are increasingly recognizing the need for sustainability and organic produce, 
we’ve seen that floral trends are beginning to follow suit,
 with increased demand for natural, earthier tones.

Neo-mint is a color that trend forecaster WGSN predicted would dominate interiors in 2021
and is described as an ‘oxygenating, fresh tone.'
 
Dried-flower arrangements make a sweet statement all year long. 
Their rustic quality and stunning texture make them an especially lovely accent for the fall,
and we scoured the market for contemporary florists innovating on the tried and true.
These florals are just as beautiful as their fresh counterparts
and don’t require going faux, but are virtually maintenance-free–simply pop them
in a favorite vessel and admire them for months, or even years, to come.
 
 When it comes to the arranging of flowers, simplicity is going to be key in floral
 trends 2021. Design trends show an increasing demand for stripped-back,
 minimalist compositions; so if you’re doing your own arrangements keep it simple
 and you won’t go far wrong.
 
 
 Loving the dried flower trend right now! They say these flowers got popular because people want those muted colors and antique look, so they turned to dried flowers and we are loving it! Title: How to preserve real flowers 🌷 | Drying flowers in seconds 😉 #diy #arthacks #flowerpressing
Description:
Preserve your flowers using this easy DIY trick 😉
Using Ironing method to preserve real flowers retaining their colour and essence 

Method: Place flowers between sheets of paper and press them with a warm iron for a few seconds to minutes. The heat quickly dries and flattens the flowers.

Uses:
   Fast Crafting: Ideal for projects that require quickly preserved flowers.
   Decorative Art: Use ironed flowers in artwork, card-making, or as embellishments.
   DIY Home Décor: Create pressed flower displays for home decoration.

Title: Top Tips For Becoming A Sustainable Florist
Description:
In this video, I share with you top tips for becoming a sustainable florist.

Key to a sustainable floral practice is using more locally grown plants and foliage in your flower creations, extending your natural season by using everlastings and of course avoiding use of "single use" plastics like floral foam and other floral accessories which cannot be broken down or re-used.

🌼 Every flower tells a story, but what about the tale of their journey from seed to bouquet? It's time we talk about sustainable floristry! 💚🌏

Sustainable floristry is more than just a trend, it's a commitment to reducing environmental impact and promoting ethical practices in flower farming and design. From local sourcing, seasonality, and biodegradable packaging, to minimizing chemical usage and waste, every step counts. 

When we choose sustainable floristry, we're not only brightening our homes or events with beautiful blooms, we're also supporting fair trade, nurturing biodiversity, and helping to preserve our planet for future generations.

The result is that agriculture can be made accessible,

Title: Mayesh Design Star: Artful & Sustainable Floral Arch
Description:
In her second Design Star video, Sue designs a floral arch with a focus on doing more with less - and not necessarily fewer flowers, but fewer varieties and elements. This practice has helped Sue become more flexible with her designs and allows her to work with the seasons and learn to design with what is available.

The armature for this design is a repurposed bundle of smilax vine that Sue has used and reused for years, which she hung from the ceiling with twine. She then chose a limited selection of five floral elements to include in her hanging arch (listed below).

💚🌷 Winter Cut Flower Arrangement: Local Seasonal Flowers + Sustainable Floristry! February 2022 🌷💚
Description:
Welcome to my channel Nicolie Flower House!  My name is Terren, and I am a cut flower grower and eco-florist.  Nicolie Flower House is located on a 1 acre suburban home backyard in zone 7b, where backyard flowers are taken to another level.  Come see how these local cut flowers are grown in raised, no dig beds throughout a potager style garden naturally, without pesticides or chemical fertilizers!  I grow intensively for maximum cut flower production and beauty.  Achieving a gravity fed rainwater irrigation system is goal of mine here at Nicolie Flower House that I plan to work on throughout this year.  I also hope to improve my succession planting in hopes of a continuous cut flower harvest during the entire season.  Soil and plant health are my top priorities.  From how I grow my flowers to how I turn them into works of art, every detail is measured against this standard.  Our planet is at the heart of everything I do. 

I am also working to innovate new ways to create sustainable floral design.  I also hope to showcase how beautiful flowers can be using sustainable methods and to make it mainstream to work with flowers in this way.  I grow all of my own flowers making them seasonal, local, and clean.  It is with much gratitude that I share my progress and designs with the you.  Thank you so much for your interest!

Title: zero waste sunflower! #shorts
Description:
You might think each sunflower is just a single bloom, but each flowerhead is actually made up of up to two thousand florets. These tiny flowers are packed full of nectar, making them a great treat for bees as well.

Almost every part of this flower is edible including young leaves which can be cooked like spinach and the whole head could be roasted when still fresh.

Making butter with sunflower seeds it’s a great healthy alternative to peanut butter and you could make it savoury or sweet depending on your taste.

Title: How to Create a Sustainable Floral Arrangement #flowersofinstagram #flowerpower #flowertagram #bloom
Description:
How to Create a Sustainable Floral Arrangement #flowersofinstagram #flowerpower #flowertagram #bloom

Descriptions

Peony: Peonies are known for their large, lush, and fragrant blooms with a romantic, layered appearance. They come in a variety of colors, from soft pastels to rich burgundy. They symbolize prosperity, good fortune, and a happy life.  

Tulip: Tulips are elegant and iconic springtime flowers with a clean, cup-shaped bloom. They are available in a vast array of colors, each with its own meaning. They symbolize perfect love and new beginnings.  

Ranunculus: Often mistaken for a small rose or peony, ranunculus flowers have numerous paper-thin petals that unfurl in a continuous spiral. They represent charm and are a popular choice for romantic and elegant bouquets.
Anemone: Anemones are distinguished by their bold, often black, central disc surrounded by a ring of brightly colored petals. They symbolize anticipation and are a perfect choice for modern and minimalist floral designs.  
Carnation: Carnations are known for their ruffled, frilly petals and long-lasting blooms. They come in many colors, each carrying a different meaning, such as love, fascination, and distinction.  

Dahlia: Dahlias are a diverse and vibrant flower with intricate petal patterns. They are available in a spectrum of colors, making them a popular choice for adding a touch of drama and lushness to any arrangement.

Title: Podcast: Sustainable Floristry Panel Discussion
Description: Sustainability. It is so much more than the current buzzword and is a very important topic for our industry. Our first 2023 Mayesh Design Star, Sue Mcleary, joined Yvonne along with Holly Chapple, TJ McGrath, Debra Prinzing, Patrick Dahlson and David Dahlson. We defined what sustainability means, examined biodegradable, compostable, and reusable materials, talked about locally grown, and our experts gave tips on how we all can get started in being gentler to the earth. This episode went over an hour and we only began to scratch the surface. Title: Mayesh Design Star: Sustainable Mechanics  & Flower Pillar
Description:
In her first Design Star video, Sue introduces the topic of Sustainable Floristry and discusses the responsibility florists have to be aware of their environmental impact and ways to reduce our footprint. We truly couldn't have asked for a better introduction because Sue so perfectly articulated our thoughts on Sustainability and aligns very closely with our philosophy. 

The goal of this year is to make this big, intimidating, somewhat "trendy" topic feel more inclusive & accessible to all florists, and give tangible approaches and ideas on how to make small changes within your business. We are well aware that being 100% sustainable is nearly impossible, but that shouldn't scare us away from trying at all. Incremental changes and an awareness of our impacts on the environment are the best way to start, and we hope to inspire florists to join the movement in any capacity!

To start us off, Sue dives right into some tangible solutions by introducing her favorite sustainable mechanics, ranging from tiny pin frogs to larger options for big arrangements. She then demonstrates designing a "Floral Pillar" using a large reusable mechanic that she had custom welded to be used over & over. 

For a list of all of the mechanics featured in this video, check out our blog: https://blog.mayesh.com/mds-sustainable-mechanics-floral-pillar

Creating a concrete baluster railing is a straightforward process with the right concrete molds and technique. Start by selecting high-quality railing molds designed for durability and detail. Mix up a strong concrete mix, ensuring the right water-to-cement ratio for strength and smooth flow.

Pour the mix into the baluster molds, making sure to fill every detail. Use a vibrating table or tap the sides of the molds to eliminate air bubbles and ensure a solid finish. Let the concrete cure properly before stripping the molds off carefully to avoid damage. Once cured, assemble the balusters into a railing system for a custom DIY concrete look that’s both durable and stylish.

Title: Why Gen Z & Millennials Are Ditching Imported Flowers for Sustainable Bouquets (Eco Wedding Tips)
Description:
Discover how Gen Z and millennials are revolutionizing weddings by growing their own flowers, sourcing locally, and cutting carbon emissions from imported blooms. In this video, we explore Emily Day's inspiring story of creating stunning, low-cost dried flower arrangements in Calgary, Canada, that lasted beyond her October wedding.
Learn the hidden environmental costs of the global flower industry, including high-carbon plane transport and harmful monoculture practices, as explained by sustainability expert Kai Chan. See why local farming and wild foraging offer beautiful, planet-friendly alternatives that save money—Emily spent just $925 CAD versus thousands on florists.
Get practical tips from pros like Holly Lukasiewicz of District 2 Florals on composting, avoiding non-biodegradable foam, and embracing seasonal selections. Plus, hear from Debra Prinzing of Slow Flowers Society on how local blooms add meaningful stories to events. Avoid fake plastic flowers; they're often not truly sustainable.
Perfect for eco-conscious couples planning weddings or events. Watch to start your sustainable floral journey today!

Modern flower gardens focus on harmony rather than perfection, embracing a soft, natural aesthetic that feels relaxing yet stylish. Whether in large yards or small balconies, thoughtful flower arrangements, seasonal planning, and mindful gardening rituals transform everyday spaces into personal retreats. This approach proves that home gardens can be productive, beautiful, and deeply restorative—nurturing both the soul and the planet at the same time.
