Title: Seasonal Gardening Secrets for Sustainable Joy
Description:
Gardening is the practice of cultivating and tending to plants for aesthetic, recreational, culinary, or environmental purposes. It is a deeply rewarding and enriching activity that connects individuals with nature, promotes well-being, and contributes to sustainable living. Here's an overview of gardening:



1. Origins and History:
Gardening has ancient roots, dating back thousands of years to early human civilizations that cultivated plants for food, medicine, and cultural significance. Throughout history, gardening has evolved from subsistence farming and ornamental gardens of royalty to a widespread hobby enjoyed by people of all ages and backgrounds.


2. Types of Gardening:
Gardening encompasses a wide range of practices and approaches, including:


Vegetable Gardening: Growing fruits, vegetables, and herbs for personal consumption or community gardens.
Flower Gardening: Cultivating ornamental plants, flowers, and shrubs for aesthetic beauty and landscaping.
Herb Gardening: Growing culinary and medicinal herbs for cooking, herbal remedies, and aromatherapy.
Container Gardening: Growing plants in containers, pots, or small spaces like balconies, patios, or windowsills.
Indoor Gardening: Cultivating plants indoors, including houseplants, terrariums, and hydroponic systems.
Specialized Gardening: Pursuing specific interests such as bonsai, succulents, bonsai, or native plants, among others.
3. Benefits of Gardening:
Gardening offers numerous physical, mental, and emotional benefits, including:


Physical Health: Gardening provides exercise, improves dexterity and mobility, and encourages outdoor activity and vitamin D exposure.
Mental Well-being: Gardening reduces stress, anxiety, and depression, promotes mindfulness, and fosters a sense of accomplishment and purpose.
Environmental Impact: Gardening supports biodiversity, conservation efforts, and sustainable living practices by creating habitats for pollinators, reducing carbon footprint, and conserving water resources.
Community Engagement: Gardening fosters social connections, community engagement, and shared learning experiences through garden clubs, workshops, and volunteer opportunities.
4. Essential Elements of Gardening:
Successful gardening requires attention to several key elements, including:


Soil Preparation: Ensuring proper soil composition, fertility, drainage, and pH levels for healthy plant growth.
Watering and Irrigation: Providing adequate moisture and irrigation to plants based on their water requirements and environmental conditions.
Sunlight and Exposure: Selecting suitable plant varieties and locations that receive appropriate sunlight exposure for optimal growth.
Pest and Disease Management: Monitoring plants for pests, diseases, and environmental stressors, and employing organic or integrated pest management strategies to mitigate risks.
Seasonal Maintenance: Performing regular tasks such as pruning, weeding, fertilizing, mulching, and seasonal planting to maintain garden health and vitality.
5. Gardening Trends and Innovations:
Gardening trends evolve over time, influenced by cultural shifts, technological advancements, and environmental concerns. Current trends include:


Urban Gardening: Growing food and plants in urban environments, including rooftop gardens, community plots, and vertical gardening systems.
Sustainability: Embracing eco-friendly practices such as composting, rainwater harvesting, organic gardening, and native plant landscaping.
Edible Landscaping: Incorporating edible plants into ornamental landscapes, blending aesthetics with functionality and food production.
Smart Gardening: Leveraging technology such as smart irrigation systems, weather sensors, gardening apps, and online resources to enhance gardening success and efficiency.
In summary, gardening is a deeply rewarding and versatile activity that enriches lives, promotes well-being, and fosters connections with nature. Whether tending to a small container garden on a balcony or cultivating a vast landscape, gardening offers endless opportunities for creativity, learning, and enjoyment, making it a beloved pastime cherished by millions around the world.

How To Have An Eco-Friendly Wedding (Tips From A Sustainable Wedding Planner)
Description:
How To Have An Eco-Friendly Wedding (Tips From A Sustainable Wedding Planner)

"Sustainability isn’t just a trend—it’s the future of events. Here’s how we made a difference this World Environment Day! 🌍

🌿 Eco-Friendly Event Ideas

1. Digital Invitations
Use e-invites, QR code RSVPs, or beautifully designed WhatsApp invitations to reduce paper waste.

2. Green Venue Selection
Choose venues with natural lighting, ventilation, or outdoor spaces to reduce energy use.
Look for LEED-certified or eco-conscious locations.

3. Reusable Décor Elements
Use fabric drapes, brass or wooden frames, cane or bamboo props, and dried florals.
Rent or repurpose décor instead of buying new for every event.

4. Floral with a Purpose
Use potted plants, succulents, or locally sourced flowers.
Donate post-event florals to hospitals or compost them.
5. Sustainable Catering

Source food locally and seasonally.
Avoid plastic cutlery and switch to biodegradable disposables or reusable tableware.

6. Zero-Waste Food Counters
Design your menu to avoid excess food. Arrange for leftover food donation via NGOs.
Avoid individual plastic water bottles — use water stations with glass or copper dispensers.

7. Eco-friendly Guest Favors
Offer seed paper invites, plantable giveaways, handmade candles, jute bags, or eco-kits.

8. Green Gifting Stations
Create a gifting area where guests choose sustainable gift options instead of mass giveaways.
9. Digital Signage & QR Programs
Display event schedules and menus digitally on screens or QR codes instead of printed standees.

10. Sustainable Fashion
Encourage guests to upcycle or repeat their looks.
Collaborate with designers using natural fabrics or recycled materials.

11. Carbon Offset Contributions
Allocate a part of the event budget to plant trees or support carbon offset programs.

12. Eco-conscious Vendor Selection
Work with vendors who follow green practices—florists using foam-free arrangements, caterers with composting systems, etc.

13. Transport Planning
Offer group shuttles, encourage carpooling, or provide e-vehicles for guest transfers.

14. Minimalist Invitations or Event Kits
Design elegant, minimal paper kits with recycled material and soy-based inks if physical material is needed.

15. Green Certification for Events
Promote that your event is certified or verified by a local green body or NGO to increase awareness and credibility.

Title: Sustainable Elegance: The Rise of Simulation Handmade Flowers in Eco-Friendly Decoration
Description:
This video is mainly to show our products to you. Include #artificialflowers #artificialflowerswholesale #wholesalesilkflowers #wholesaleartificialflowers #artificialflowersarrangements and so on.


Welcome to the world of sustainable elegance where beauty and ethics go hand in hand. As people become more conscious about their impact on the environment, there has been a rise in interest towards eco-friendly options for every aspect of our lives. This shift is also reflected in interior aesthetics and decoration, with an increasing demand for sustainably-made products that add charm and sophistication to living spaces without harming the planet. In particular, simulated handmade flowers have emerged as a top choice for those seeking stylish yet environmentally friendly decor. In this blog post, we will dive into the rising trend of simulation handmade flowers and explore why they are becoming a must-have element in any eco-conscious home's interior design scheme.

How to Make a Bohemian Bridal Bouquet
Description:
Creating a bridal bouquet involves selecting flowers, choosing a color scheme, and ensuring the bouquet complements the wedding theme. This color palette is based on a currently trending natural or Bohemian look. Join Sandy Schroeck as she goes through the techniques needed to create a stunning spiral-style bridal bouquet. Start by preparing the flowers by removing any excess foliage below the binding point. Begin with a focal flower or armature and build around it, adding different types of flowers and greenery to create depth and texture. DIY Bridal Bouquet with Faux Flowers | How I made a faux floral bridal bouquet for a Summer Wedding
Description:
This video walks through my process of making my first ever faux floral bouquet!

Fresh flower bouquets for weddings can be PRICEY, so we decided that a faux floral bouquet that could be used on two dates— for bridal portraits and for the wedding— would be perfect!

Items needed: 
- Faux florals - All the ones I purchased were from Hobby Lobby and were 50% off. I highly recommend shopping florals during sales like these for more savings!
- Floral stem tape
- Hot glue gun 
- Wire Cutters
- Ribbon for wrapping stems
- Scissors

The written directions can be found on my blog page, linked here: https://poplinandpine.com/2023/01/01/diy-bridal-bouquet-with-faux-flowers/

Want to book your bridal photos with me in DFW? Request a date here: https://ansleystringer1.sproutstudio.com/book/6388eb2c5b70e5.74897667

Creating a DIY faux floral bouquet allows you to customize every flower and every color that goes into it. They’re durable and can be used for multiple photo sessions!

Title: How to Open a Flower Shop Right | Six Things to do Before Starting
Description:
For more information about how to open a flower shop check out https://openaflowershop.com. 

Have more questions?  Ask in the comments!
Here are six things you need to do before starting a flower shop.  

Number 1 - Volunteer to work in a flower shop.  Even if you have arranged flowers in the past, it’s still a good idea to see how different shops operate and look over the owner’s shoulder to see what goes on behind the scenes.  Look for a shop far enough away from where you plan to open so that you aren’t a threat to their business.  There have been too many people who had this dream of quitting their job to start a flower shop and then spend their life savings to find out it wasn’t as fun as they thought it would be.  

Number 2 - Go to an industry related conference to learn more about what’s happening in the industry and network with people who are doing what you want to do.  This is a great opportunity to learn from their successes and failures and get advice for what you should be focusing on.   These conferences are also great because you get to see many of the vendors and look at the products to sell in your shop.  The Society of American Florists and International Floriculture Expo hold some of the largest events every year.  The cost to attend isn’t cheap but the knowledge you can gain is more than worth it.  

Number 3 - Get your plan written down.  Write a business plan, no matter how informal, to get the ideas out of your head and on to paper.  Sometimes things make sense in your head but when you write them down, it’s easier to see flaws.  Share the plan with friends and mentors to get different perspectives to be sure you haven’t missed anything.  Also be sure to spend some time on the numbers to see if this shop is feasible.  There are free and paid business plan templates online or you can get help from a Small Business Development Center or SCORE for free help with your business plan. 

Number 4 – Educate yourself on efficient production and inventory management.  Flower shops are very fast paced during the big holidays like Valentine’s Day and Mother’s Day and you need a plan to get good seasonal people who can help when things get too busy for you to handle.  If you are betting on just having friends and family help, be careful as they may not be there when you need them.  Also, what is your plan for managing inventory and how will you balance the need to order for big holidays but not have too much left over to spoil?  Flowers are perishable and their lifespan isn’t very long.  Once they start to go bad, they aren’t sellable and there is no getting that money back. 

Number 5 – Get your marketing plan in order.  Why are people going to buy from you?  What makes your shop better than the other options in your community?  How are people going to find you?  Many new store owners don’t want to budget much money to advertise and expect the sign on the front of the building along some social media will take care of their promotions.  While there are some inexpensive options to market, those options take time and need to be consistent if they are going to be successful.  Will you have the time for social media in addition to running the shop?  Last, what is your plan to market online?  Online only stores continue to take sales from retail stores and just creating a website for your store isn’t going to be enough.  If people can’t find you online, you will be missing out on a considerable amount of online sales.  Not sure how to do this?  Start talking now with a digital marketing company to find the options and costs. 

Number 6 – Work your connections.  Do you have connections with funeral homes and wedding related businesses that your competitors don’t?  Funerals and weddings typically make up over 30% of income for most flower shops and the volume of sales from these sources is often an indicator of whether a flower shop will be profitable or not.  If you are in an area that has a competitor who has these locked up, you will be starting at a huge disadvantage.  

Well, there you have it, six things you need to do before starting a flower shop.  Running a flower shop is so much more than making nice flower arrangements and many people are not fully prepared for that reality.  The good news is that anyone can learn, but be sure to take your time and get educated before investing your time and money to get started.  

For more information about opening your flower shop, head over to Openaflowershop.com to get more tips and advice.

Title: Want a Successful Flower Shop? Avoid These 5 Mistakes!
Description:
💐 Running a successful flower shop isn’t just about what you do—it’s about what you AVOID doing! 

Here are my top 5 business no-gos that have kept my shop blooming:

1️⃣ Ignoring Customer Feedback – Whether it’s praise or criticism, feedback is a GOLDEN opportunity for improvement. 🌟
2️⃣ Using Outdated Technology – Staying up to date with POS and inventory systems saves time and frustration. ⏳
3️⃣ Ignoring Team Feedback – Happy employees = happy customers. I always involve my team in decisions. 🤝
4️⃣ Skipping Staff Training – A knowledgeable, confident team provides the best customer experience. 🌸
5️⃣ Neglecting Shop Aesthetics – First impressions matter! A clean, organized, and welcoming space keeps customers coming back. ✨

How to Start a Flower Shop | How to Open a Floral Business | How to Start a Flower Business |Florist
Description:
How to Start a Flower Shop? Or, How to Open a Floral Business? Or, How to Start a Flower Business?

Starting a flower shop can be an exciting and profitable business venture, especially if you have a passion for flowers and design. However, like any business, it requires careful planning and strategy. Below, we outline the key steps to help you turn your dream of owning a flower shop into a successful reality.

1. Create a Business Plan
A solid business plan is essential when starting any business. Your plan should cover the basics: your business goals, target audience, market analysis, competition, pricing strategy, and sales projections. You should also outline your startup costs and sources of funding. A well-thought-out business plan will not only guide your efforts but will also be necessary if you seek a loan or investment.

2. Choose the Right Location
Location plays a crucial role in the success of a flower shop. It should be in a high-traffic area, such as a busy street or shopping center, where people are likely to stop by. Consider proximity to other businesses, events, or places where flowers are commonly needed, such as hospitals, hotels, and wedding venues. Ensure your space is large enough to accommodate flower displays, storage, and workspace.

3. Obtain the Necessary Permits and Licenses
Before opening your flower shop, research the local laws and regulations regarding small businesses. You’ll likely need a business license, a sales tax permit, and possibly an employee identification number (EIN) from the IRS. Depending on your location, you might also need a health or safety permit if you are selling food-related products or dealing with perishable goods.

4. Source Your Flowers and Supplies
Establish relationships with reliable flower wholesalers or local flower growers. Your flowers should always be fresh, as quality is key in this business. Along with flowers, you’ll need to stock up on supplies like vases, floral foam, ribbons, and other decorations. You can purchase these items in bulk to save costs.

5. Create a Pricing Strategy
Your pricing should be competitive but also reflect the quality of your flowers and service. Consider your costs, including flowers, labor, utilities, and rent, to determine your pricing structure. Offering packages for events like weddings or corporate gatherings can help attract larger orders and boost your revenue.

6. Design Your Shop and Showcase Your Flowers
The look and feel of your shop are crucial for attracting customers. The arrangement of flowers and decorations should be eye-catching and inviting. Consider creating a display area near the entrance that draws people in. Use your design skills to create beautiful, seasonal arrangements that will inspire customers.

7. Develop an Online Presence
In today's digital age, an online presence is a must. Create a website with an online store for customers to place orders. Use social media platforms like Instagram and Facebook to showcase your arrangements, share flower care tips, and promote special offers. An online presence will help increase brand visibility and reach a broader audience.

8. Hire and Train Your Staff
If you plan to hire employees, choose people who are passionate about flowers and have a good eye for design. Properly train them in customer service, floral arrangement techniques, and handling flowers. Happy, knowledgeable employees are key to creating a positive customer experience.

9. Market Your Flower Shop
Effective marketing is essential to growing your flower shop business. Utilize both online and offline methods, including local SEO, flyers, social media ads, and partnerships with event planners. Offering loyalty programs, seasonal discounts, and delivery services will also encourage repeat business.

Starting a flower shop requires passion, creativity, and business acumen. By following these steps, you'll be on your way to creating a successful flower shop that brings beauty and joy to your community. With careful planning, dedication, and hard work, your flower shop can bloom into a thriving business.

Pro Flower Tips: Common Mistakes To Avoid
Description:
Hello Flower Friends! I'm Ellen. Sign up for my FREE weekly newsletter (https://www.ellenfrost.com/join-now) for more flower fun! I write flower profiles, chronicle my flower adventures and share flower stories.
 
In this video I identify common floral design mistakes and how to fix them.

The first mistake I see all the time among inexperienced designers is not cleaning the stems. All of the stems need to be stripped of leaves below the opening of the vase. Leaves inside of the vase look messy and unprofessional. There is also a functional reason to clean the stems. Leaves in the vase can contaminate the water with dirt and bacteria which reduces the overall vase life of the flowers.

Another common error is designing without the viewer in mind. It can be easy to fixate on one side of an arrangement or ignore perspective. Designers are looking down on the arrangement as we work. Be sure to turn the arrangement while working and consider the eye level of the viewer.

Varying the heights of the flowers is an technique that shows you're a pro. New designers often create static arrangements by cutting all stems the same length. Create dynamism and interest while showing off focal flowers by layering blooms at different heights.

Don't stuff your vase. Over-stuffing is common mistake by over-zealous designers. I know, I get excited too by all of the beautiful flowers. But, flowers need space to be seen and to move.

Finally, you got to maintain. Cut any failing blooms and pull dead stems out of the arrangement. Change the water daily and give the entire arrangement a fresh cut ever few days. This will keep your design looking good longer.

Title: Billion Dollar Flower Market | ENDEVR Documentary
Description:
Billion Dollar Flower Market | Business Documentary from 2016

The Flower industry is a billionaire global business. The Netherlands is the world’s most advanced flower producer and controls the market. Nearly all of the flowers that the United Kingdom buy are from abroad and it’s the Dutch. But there’s a chink in the Dutch armour and the Brits are fighting back. 

This documentary follows florist Geoff, who learned Dutch and buys directly at the flower auctions in Holland, passing on savings to his loyal customers back home.  We meet a new generation of British florists including 21-year-old Jay, whose new business is booming because of a dedication to using quirky homegrown British buds for her luscious event displays. Florists like Jay are even helping keep the world famous flower market Covent Garden in cheeky rude health. And it’s not only them. Our high street supermarket giants have realized growing business opportunities, spotting how to encourage shoppers to fill their trollies with homegrown Sweet Williams sourced from local super grower Mark. And we reveal just how florist Rob did it to create his huge flower display outside London’s National Portrait Gallery. 

Our own humble British flowers and enterprising British florists are on a roll, the Dutch aren’t having it all their own way.

Title: The Billion Dollar Flower Market: Behind the Scenes of a Global Business | FD Finance
Description:
Billion Dollar Flower Market | FD Finance 

Watch: 'The Price we Pay' here: https://youtu.be/k76f8Iaac5A

The Flower industry is a billionaire global business. The Netherlands is the world’s most advanced flower producer and controls the market. Nearly all of the flowers that the United Kingdom buy are from abroad and it’s the Dutch. But there’s a chink in the Dutch armour and the Brits are fighting back. This documentary follows florist Geoff, who learned Dutch and buys directly at the flower auctions in Holland, passing on savings to his loyal customers back home.  We meet a new generation of British florists including 21-year-old Jay, whose new business is booming because of a dedication to using quirky homegrown British buds for her luscious event displays. Florists like Jay are even helping keep the world famous flower market Covent Garden in cheeky rude health. And it’s not only them. Our high street supermarket giants have realized growing business opportunities, spotting how to encourage shoppers to fill their trollies with homegrown Sweet Williams sourced from local super grower Mark. And we reveal just how florist Rob did it to create his huge flower display outside London’s National Portrait Gallery. Our own humble British flowers and enterprising British florists are on a roll, the Dutch aren’t having it all their own way.

Title: Bogota in BLOOM — Proflora 2025 Showcases Colombia’s Floral Magic
Description:
Bogota has come alive with color as Proflora 2025, Colombia’s premier floriculture fair, opened its doors to producers, buyers, and flower enthusiasts from around the world. Running from October first to third, the international event highlights Colombia’s position as a global leader in flower production and export. Visitors were treated to stunning displays of roses, orchids, carnations, and tropical blooms — many cultivated in Colombia’s fertile highlands. Proflora 2025 not only celebrates the artistry of floral design but also serves as a key platform for trade, innovation, and sustainability in the global flower industry. Exhibitors from multiple countries gathered to network and showcase the latest trends in cultivation and logistics. The Bogota flower fair once again proved why Colombia remains the heart of the world’s floriculture.

Title: How Do Wedding Florists Set Up So Many Flowers? - Wedding Trends Now
Description:
How Do Wedding Florists Set Up So Many Flowers? In this engaging video, we’ll take you behind the scenes of wedding floristry. Discover the meticulous planning and teamwork that goes into creating stunning floral displays for weddings. We’ll walk you through the essential steps florists take to ensure that every bloom is perfect for the big day. From the initial flower orders to the final arrangements, we’ll cover how florists prepare and condition flowers, manage their workspace, and coordinate with venues and other vendors.

Title: Top 2025 Trends in Decor, Fashion, and Entertainment Revealed
Description:
Welcome to Curious Minds! In this video, we explore the top trends shaping 2025 across various industries.​

Spring Decor Trends: Maximalism is making a comeback in spring 2025 decor, featuring bold patterns, statement furniture, and rich textures. Incorporate floral wall art and oversized cushions to refresh your home this season. ​

Fashion and Beauty: Accessible luxury is on the rise, with items like Coach's Quilted Tabby Bag leading the way. Ballet flats and kitten heels are making a strong comeback, offering comfort and elegance. In beauty, consumers are gravitating towards wellness-focused products, including under-eye brighteners and peel-off lip stains. 
Entertainment: The awards season has sparked massive online discussions, with events like the Oscars drawing record numbers of searches. Streaming platforms continue to dominate, with users flocking to Netflix and Disney+ for the latest hit shows. Music festivals and concerts are also trending, with fans eager to attend live events.​

Cryptocurrency: Cryptocurrency remains a hot topic in 2025, with increased interest in digital currencies like Bitcoin and Ethereum. More people are exploring crypto investments and the world of decentralized finance (DeFi).​

Emerging Tech Trends: Artificial Intelligence (AI) and Machine Learning (ML) are advancing rapidly, automating tasks and enhancing customer experiences. Quantum Computing is on the horizon, promising to revolutionize problem-solving across various sectors. The Internet of Things (IoT) is expanding, connecting devices for smarter living. Augmented Reality (AR) and Virtual Reality (VR) are becoming more mainstream, offering immersive experiences in education and entertainment. The global rollout of 5G networks is enabling innovations like autonomous vehicles and real-time data processing. Blockchain Technology is gaining traction beyond cryptocurrencies, enhancing transparency and security in various applications.

Title: Child labour behind global brands' best-selling perfumes - BBC World Service Documentaries
Description:
A new investigation from BBC Eye takes you to the heart of the jasmine trade in Egypt, to reveal the dark secrets behind the perfume industry.

Click here to subscribe to our channel 👉🏽 https://bbc.in/3VyyriM

Children are working in dangerous conditions throughout the night, and pickers are often earning as little as a dollar a day, while huge profits are made by the giants in the perfume industry.  

Over half of the world’s supply of jasmine is produced in Egypt and this story reveals the true human cost of this precious flower, considered to be one of the most valuable ingredients used in some of the world's most iconic perfumes.  

Billion-dollar global brands, who use Egyptian jasmine in their perfumes, claim to have zero tolerance on child labour throughout their supply chains. But the BBC can reveal that in the 2023 jasmine harvest season, children - some as young as five years old - were working in the jasmine fields that were supplying these global brands through factories in Egypt.

Description:

viral Flower Market is returning bigger and better than ever, this time at 

At Flower Market 2.0, there will be 27 brand-new plush flower designs. Pick your first flower for free, and purchase additional ones for $5 each.

Asia's Top Flower Market Blooms on Growing Demand
Description:
#kechhotline 

Early mornings at the Dounan Flower Market in southwest China's Kunming City are vibrant and bustling. Spanning eight square kilometers, Dounan is Asia's largest fresh-cut flower market, drawing buyers from all over the country. At the auction center, which operates like a stock exchange, buyers bid on various flower varieties and prices. To meet the overwhelming demand, officials have added another trading session, much to the delight of the eager buyers.

Title: Shop the NYC Flower Market with Lewis Miller for Mother's Day
Description:
Renowned floral designer Lewis Miller celebrates Mother’s Day by shopping the NYC Flower Market, then returns to his studio to create three companion floral arrangements inspired by his favorite vessels and printed textiles.

For more extraordinary home tours, visit our website: homeworthy.com

In this special Mother’s Day episode of Homeworthy, join renowned floral designer Lewis Miller—celebrated for his transformative Flower Flashes—as he takes us behind the scenes on a blooming adventure through the NYC Flower Market, selecting the season’s most beautiful stems to honor the mothers in our lives.

Back at his studio, Lewis shares his expert eye for vessels, demonstrating how to choose and pair them to create three smaller-scale, companion arrangements that feel cohesive and elevated. With a curated display of favorite vessels, he selects a trio that work harmoniously, revealing his process for designing florals that complement one another in size, texture, and tone.

Lewis also opens up about his love of textiles, showing how a vibrant printed tablecloth can spark a floral color story, inspire creativity, and elevate any tablescape. This episode is full of heartfelt ideas and design inspiration for making Mother’s Day truly special.

Title: 🎄🌹Christmas at the San Francisco Flower Market🎄🌹
Description:
🎄🌹 Step inside the San Francisco Flower Mart at Christmas time for a magical behind-the-scenes tour of one of the West Coast’s most iconic wholesale flower markets. In this video, I’m taking you along as we explore the Flower Mart dressed for the holidays—overflowing with fresh Christmas flowers, winter greenery, holiday décor, and seasonal floral inspiration.
You’ll see custom evergreen garlands, berries, novelty holiday blooms, and stunning floral supplies that florists and designers use to create unforgettable Christmas arrangements, wreaths, and centerpieces. Whether you’re a professional florist, a flower lover, or simply looking for holiday decorating ideas, this festive market tour is full of inspiration.
The San Francisco Flower Mart, founded in 1912, has been a cornerstone of the city’s floral industry for over a century—supplying designers, events, and shops with world-class blooms from around the globe. During the holidays, it becomes especially magical, blending old-world charm with modern Christmas flair.

.🎄🌹 In this video you’ll see:

[BANGKOK] Bangkok Flower Market (Pak Khlong Talat) "The Largest Flower Market In Thailand" [4K HDR]
Description:
Bangkok Flower Market (Pak Khlong Talad) is the biggest wholesale and retail fresh flower market in Bangkok. The market has all kinds of popular flowers and flora-related items, including roses, forget-me-nots, orchids, lilies and more. Most of them are sold in packs of 50 or 100 flowers in each, and prices are amazingly cheap.

The market is part of the Old City and is located on Chak Phet Road, near Saphan Phut (Memorial Bridge). Shops and vendors are housed inside 2- to 3-storey shophouses on both sides of the main road. The market lies just south of Wat Pho (Temple of the Reclining Buddha) and has access to a river pier, so it makes for a great single-day trip when combined with other historical attractions in the area.

Opening hours : Everyday 24 hours. (Recommend time est. 07:00 PM)

How to get there:
- MRT Sanamchai Station (BL31) Exit 5, 2-3 minutes walk
- Chao Phraya River boat: Memorial Bridge Pier (N6) or Rajinee Pier (N7), 2-3 minutes walk
- Bus: 8, 73

Title: Flower market in Guwahati makes record sales with Diwali demand surge
Description:
The wholesale flower market in Panbazar, one of the largest in Guwahati, witnessed a surge in sales during the festive season, with florists reporting “higher profits and an overwhelming response from customers.”
According to the florists of Guwahati, the demand for marigold, a traditional favorite during Diwali celebrations, skyrocketed this year. Flower vendors told Business North East that they collectively sold over five lakh marigold flowers amid Diwali festivities. “The demand not only exceeded expectations but also led to a substantial increase in profits for the flower sellers”, a local vendor informed BNE.

Florists in the market, interacting with BNE, expressed their delight over the robust sales figures. One vendor shared, "This Diwali has been exceptionally good for us. We have seen an increase in the number of customers. Demand for marigold has also been picked up due to the festive season. We managed to make profits exceeding one lakh, a considerable boost for our business."

One notable aspect of this year's Diwali flower market was the remarkable drop in prices of marigold garlands. Traditionally priced between Rs 40-60, the festive flower garlands were available at a more affordable price of Rs 20-25, contributing further to the increase in sales volume.Most of the supplies come from Kolkata, West Bengal, one of the florists informed BNE.
It was witnessed that the market was abuzz with activity throughout the festive season, attracting a larger crowd in comparison to 2022.
Flower vendors informed that there was an uptick in the number of customers. “Even though a lot of private flower shops have opened in the city and people now have a plethora of options, a huge number of customers continue to buy a large amount of flowers during festive occasions" a vendor said.

Title: Marigold Flower Farming | गेंदा फूल की खेती | Flower Market Demand & Profit | Marigold Cultivation
Description:
@ABCINDIA-AmitBhobhia 
Marigold (Tagetes) is one of the most popular and profitable flowers grown in India and worldwide. Known for its bright yellow, orange, and golden blooms, marigold farming is easy, low-cost, and gives high returns. Farmers can earn good profits by cultivating marigolds for weddings, religious functions, decorations, and festivals like Diwali, Durga Puja, and Onam.
This video/article explains the complete guide to marigold cultivation – from soil preparation, seed selection, planting, irrigation, and fertilizers to harvesting and marketing. You’ll also learn how much investment is required, expected yield per acre, and profit calculation. Marigold flowers are in high demand throughout the year, making them an excellent cash crop for small and large farmers.
If you are looking for a low-maintenance, high-income flower crop, marigold farming can be your best option! 🌸

Title: Sydney's Flemington Flower Market: Where Flowers Meet Affordable Prices!
Description:
The Sydney Flower Market is Australia’s largest flower market, bringing in over $150 million each year and covering about 75% of the wholesale cut flower trade in NSW. It mainly supplies local florists but is also open to the public, offering fresh flowers, potted plants, and florist supplies. With around 170 traders, the market gets its flowers from nearby areas, benefiting from the diverse local climates. These traders help keep prices low, making it a key spot for fresh flowers in the industry.

Columbia Road - London's Finest Flower Market, London, United Kingdom #flowers #flowermarket
Description:
A flower-lover's paradise in east London


One of London's most well-known and nicest-looking markets, Columbia Road overflows with bucketfuls of beautiful flowers every Sunday. From 8am-3pm, market traders line the narrow street selling flowers, houseplants, herbs, bulbs and shrubs.

On Sundays the street transforms into an oasis of foliage and flowers from around the world. Everything from bedding plants to ten ft banana trees can be found here, with plants and flowers grown by the sellers themselves and imported from countries across the globe.
Considered one of London's most visually appealing markets, Columbia Road overflows with bucketfuls of beautiful flowers.

Title: Thursday Flower Market in Florence, Italy
Description:
PLEASE SUPPORT US BY SUBSCRIBING - THANK YOU!

In case you're wondering, here a video of the Thursday Flower Market in Florence, Italy

#flowermarket #florence #italy #flower #market #flowers #thursdayflowermarket #mazzo #bouquet #plants #viapellicceria #piazzarepubblica #arcade

Full video can be seen here: https://youtu.be/sZlh0BSkufo

https://florence-on-line.com/markets/thursday-flower-market.html

If you find yourself in Florence centro on a Thursday morning, you may notice the beautiful flowers all the Florentines are carrying home to their palazzi. They've already been to the Thursday Flower Market - the place to go for fresh cut flowers for your table, geraniums for your windowbox, rosemary for your kitchen sill or potted olive trees for your city garden.

The selection is extensive and includes everything from herbs and flowers to palms and cactus. We also purchase our Christmas tree there each year. Even if you aren't looking to buy a "mazzo" (bunch or bouquet) of flowers, this market is worth a browse for it's shear beauty.

The flower market is located on Via Pellicceria, starting on the southwest corner of Piazza Repubblica and running south under the arcade and past the post office. The market starts early and all the cut flowers are pretty much sold out by lunch. By mid-afternoon all the vendors are packing up to head back to their farms, so go early. Open all year round.
